Whether or not an accordion is disabled from user interaction.
@defaultValue false

AccordionItem.description:
Whether or not an accordion is disabled from user interaction.
@defaultValue false

ApplicationShell9.name: preview
ApplicationShell9.description: When true, constrains the layout inside a fixed-size container so it renders inside the docs preview instead of as a full viewport app shell.

ChartCard14.name: description
ChartCard14.defaultValue: Min, max, and average response times

ChartCard9.name: description
ChartCard9.defaultValue: Visitors by device type over time

ChartTooltipContent.name: active
ChartTooltipContent.description:
If true, then Tooltip is always displayed, once an activeIndex is set by mouse over, or programmatically.
If false, then Tooltip is never displayed.
If active is undefined, Recharts will control when the Tooltip displays. This includes mouse and keyboard controls.

ChartTooltipContent.name: includeHidden
ChartTooltipContent.description: If true, then Tooltip will information about hidden series (defaults to false). Interacting with the hide property of Area, Bar, Line, Scatter.

Legend.name: margin
Legend.type: { top?: number; left?: number; bottom?: number | undefined; right?: number | undefined; } | undefined

ChartLegendContent.name: margin
ChartLegendContent.type: { top?: number; left?: number; bottom?: number | undefined; right?: number | undefined; } | undefined

ChipMenu.name: value
ChipMenu.description: Currently selected value (must match an option's value).

ChipMenu.name: placeholder
ChipMenu.description: Placeholder when no value is selected.

ChipMenu.name: className
ChipMenu.description: Optional class for the trigger badge.

ChipMenu.name: contentClassName
ChipMenu.description: Optional class for the dropdown content.

Command.name: filter
Command.description:
Custom filter function for whether each command menu item should matches the given search query.
It should return a number between 0 and 1, with 1 being the best match and 0 being hidden entirely.
By default, uses the `command-score` library.

Command.name: label
Command.description: Accessible label for this command menu. Not shown visibly.

Command.name: value
Command.description: Optional controlled state of the selected command menu item.

Command.name: shouldFilter
Command.description:
Optionally set to `false` to turn off the automatic filtering and sorting.
If `false`, you must conditionally render valid items based on the search query yourself.

Command.name: onValueChange
Command.description: Event handler called when the selected item of the menu changes.

Command.name: loop
Command.description: Optionally set to `true` to turn on looping around when using the arrow keys.

Command.name: disablePointerSelection
Command.description: Optionally set to `true` to disable selection via pointer events.

Command.name: vimBindings
Command.description: Set to `false` to disable ctrl+n/j/p/k shortcuts. Defaults to `true`.

CommandInput.name: value
CommandInput.description: Optional controlled state for the value of the search input.

CommandInput.name: onValueChange
CommandInput.description: Event handler called when the search value changes.

CommandList.name: label
CommandList.description: Accessible label for this List of suggestions. Not shown visibly.

CommandGroup.name: heading
CommandGroup.description: Optional heading to render for this group.

CommandGroup.name: value
CommandGroup.description: If no heading is provided, you must provide a value that is unique for this group.

CommandGroup.name: forceMount
CommandGroup.description: Whether this group is forcibly rendered regardless of filtering.

CommandItem.name: onSelect
CommandItem.description: Event handler for when this item is selected, either via click or keyboard selection.

CommandItem.name: disabled
CommandItem.description: Whether this item is currently disabled.

CommandItem.name: value
CommandItem.description:
A unique value for this item.
If no value is provided, it will be inferred from `children` or the rendered `textContent`. If your `textContent` changes between renders, you _must_ provide a stable, unique `value`.

CommandItem.name: forceMount
CommandItem.description: Whether this item is forcibly rendered regardless of filtering.

CommandItem.name: keywords
CommandItem.description: Optional keywords to match against when filtering.

CommandSeparator.name: alwaysRender
CommandSeparator.description: Whether this separator should always be rendered. Useful if you disable automatic filtering.

ContextView.name: link
ContextView.description: Optional link (e.g. "Optional link" with icon). Shown when variant is `full` or `withLink`.

ContextView.name: primaryAction
ContextView.description: Primary action button. Shown when variant is `full` or `withActions`.

ContextView.name: secondaryAction
ContextView.description: Secondary action button. Shown when variant is `full` or `withActions`.

Dock.name: autoHide
Dock.description: When true, dock hides off-screen and shows on edge hover.

Dock.name: magnification
Dock.description: Scale of the hovered icon (macOS-like zoom). Default 1.4.

Dock.name: hideDelay
Dock.description: Delay in ms before hiding when pointer leaves. Default 300.

Dock.name: triggerSize
Dock.description: Thickness of the edge trigger zone in px for auto-hide. Default 24.

DockItem.name: href
DockItem.description: If set, renders as a link.

Drawer.name: closeThreshold
Drawer.description:
Number between 0 and 1 that determines when the drawer should be closed.
Example: threshold of 0.5 would close the drawer if the user swiped for 50% of the height of the drawer or more.

Drawer.name: noBodyStyles
Drawer.description: When `true` the `body` doesn't get any styles assigned from Vaul

Drawer.name: setBackgroundColorOnScale
Drawer.description: When `false` we don't change body's background color when the drawer is open.

Drawer.name: scrollLockTimeout
Drawer.description: Duration for which the drawer is not draggable after scrolling content inside of the drawer.

Drawer.name: fixed
Drawer.description: When `true`, don't move the drawer upwards if there's space, but rather only change it's height so it's fully scrollable when the keyboard is open

Drawer.name: handleOnly
Drawer.description: When `true` only allows the drawer to be dragged by the `<Drawer.Handle />` component.

Drawer.name: dismissible
Drawer.description:
When `false` dragging, clicking outside, pressing esc, etc. will not close the drawer.
Use this in comination with the `open` prop, otherwise you won't be able to open/close the drawer.

Drawer.name: modal
Drawer.description: When `false` it allows to interact with elements outside of the drawer without closing it.

Drawer.name: direction
Drawer.description: Direction of the drawer. Can be `top` or `bottom`, `left`, `right`.

Drawer.name: defaultOpen
Drawer.description: Opened by default, skips initial enter animation. Still reacts to `open` state changes

Drawer.name: disablePreventScroll
Drawer.description: When set to `true` prevents scrolling on the document body on mount, and restores it on unmount.

Drawer.name: repositionInputs
Drawer.description:
When `true` Vaul will reposition inputs rather than scroll then into view if the keyboard is in the way.
Setting it to `false` will fall back to the default browser behavior.

Drawer.name: snapToSequentialPoint
Drawer.description:
Disabled velocity based swiping for snap points.
This means that a snap point won't be skipped even if the velocity is high enough.
Useful if each snap point in a drawer is equally important.

Drawer.name: onAnimationEnd
Drawer.description:
Gets triggered after the open or close animation ends, it receives an `open` argument with the `open` state of the drawer by the time the function was triggered.
Useful to revert any state changes for example.

FileUpload.name: onUpload
FileUpload.type: ((files: File[], options: { onProgress: (file: File, progress: number) => void; onSuccess: (file: File) => void; onError: (file: File, error: Error) => void; }) => void | Promise<...>)

FormField.name: render
FormField.type: ({ field, fieldState, formState, }: { field: ControllerRenderProps<TFieldValues, TName>; fieldState: ControllerFieldState; formState: UseFormStateReturn<TFieldValues>; }) => ReactElement<...>

AppLink.name: onClick
AppLink.description: Optional event handler for when Link is clicked.

AppLink.name: onMouseEnter
AppLink.description: Optional event handler for when the mouse pointer is moved onto Link

AppLink.name: onTouchStart
AppLink.description: Optional event handler for when Link is touched.

AppLink.name: href
AppLink.description:
The path or URL to navigate to. It can also be an object.
@example https://nextjs.org/docs/api-reference/next/link#with-url-object

AppLink.name: replace
AppLink.description:
Replace the current `history` state instead of adding a new url into the stack.
@defaultValue `false`

AppLink.name: as
AppLink.description: Optional decorator for the path that will be shown in the browser URL bar. Before Next.js 9.5.3 this was used for dynamic routes, check our [previous docs](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/v9.5.2/docs/api-reference/next/link.md#dynamic-routes) to see how it worked. Note: when this path differs from the one provided in `href` the previous `href`/`as` behavior is used as shown in the [previous docs](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/blob/v9.5.2/docs/api-reference/next/link.md#dynamic-routes).

AppLink.name: scroll
shallow
AppLink.description:
Update the path of the current page without rerunning [`getStaticProps`](https://nextjs.org/docs/pages/building-your-application/data-fetching/get-static-props), [`getServerSideProps`](https://nextjs.org/docs/pages/building-your-application/data-fetching/get-server-side-props) or [`getInitialProps`](/docs/pages/api-reference/functions/get-initial-props).
@defaultValue `false`

AppLink.name: passHref
AppLink.description:
Forces `Link` to send the `href` property to its child.
@defaultValue `false`

AppLink.name: prefetch
AppLink.description:
Prefetch the page in the background.
Any `<Link />` that is in the viewport (initially or through scroll) will be prefetched.
Prefetch can be disabled by passing `prefetch={false}`. Prefetching is only enabled in production.

In App Router:
- "auto", null, undefined (default): For statically generated pages, this will prefetch the full React Server Component data. For dynamic pages, this will prefetch up to the nearest route segment with a [`loading.js`](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/loading) file. If there is no loading file, it will not fetch the full tree to avoid fetching too much data.
- `true`: This will prefetch the full React Server Component data for all route segments, regardless of whether they contain a segment with `loading.js`.
- `false`: This will not prefetch any data, even on hover.

In Pages Router:
- `true` (default): The full route & its data will be prefetched.
- `false`: Prefetching will not happen when entering the viewport, but will still happen on hover.
@defaultValue `true` (pages router) or `null` (app router)

AppLink.name: locale
AppLink.description:
The active locale is automatically prepended. `locale` allows for providing a different locale.
When `false` `href` has to include the locale as the default behavior is disabled.
Note: This is only available in the Pages Router.

AppLink.name: legacyBehavior
onNavigate
AppLink.description: Optional event handler for when the `<Link>` is navigated.

List.name: header
List.description: Optional header content (e.g. title) rendered above the list
